Name: Ryan Fletcher
Summary: Ryan is looking to solve a critical follow-up process breakdown affecting 60-80 weekly leads with an 11-12% conversion rate. He has MD approval and a budget allocated, with a hard deadline of end of July for implementation. He's already evaluated HubSpot, found it too complex, and is actively moving toward a decision on Vantage CRM's Salesforce-integrated solution.
Next Steps: Send one-pager and proposal today to ryan.fletcher@trenthamco.co.uk | Include head of onboarding contact information | Schedule joint call with Ryan and MD for early next week | Prepare implementation timeline for 2-3 week onboarding | Confirm annual pricing at £11,500 is acceptable before call
Why: High budget approval, clear authority (with MD co-sign), urgent quantified need (lost leads worth £70k+ annually), tight timeline (end of July), and strong engagement with buying intent already expressed.

Name: Sarah Bennett
Summary: Sarah Bennett, Operations Director at a 12-person recruitment agency scaling to 20, has a clear pain point: manual data entry and lost follow-ups costing placements. She has budget allocated for tools, pricing is within her range (£540/month), and she's scheduled a demo for Thursday 2pm with team lead Marcus to evaluate FlowState CRM as a replacement for their fragmented Bullhorn/spreadsheet setup.
Next Steps: Send calendar invite for Thursday 2pm demo to sarah.bennett@bennettclarke.co.uk | Prepare recruitment-focused demo with req-to-placement workflows and automated follow-up examples | Research current Bullhorn configuration to understand migration complexity | Have rollout and onboarding strategy ready for Marcus (team lead managing implementation)
Why: Strong budget confirmation (within allocation), clear decision-maker with budget authority, acute urgent pain (lost placements), imminent timeline (demo Thursday), and high engagement with specific use-case focus.

Name: Daniel
Summary: James called Daniel at Pryce Talent to introduce FlowState CRM ahead of their Vincere contract renewal (end 2026/early 2027). Daniel agreed to receive materials but showed minimal enthusiasm and uncertain on exact renewal timing. This was a brief, transactional conversation with limited discovery.
Next Steps: Send brief CRM overview materials to info@prycetalent.co.uk | Create reminder to follow up Q4 2026 when contract renewal approaches | On next contact, clarify decision-maker and budget authority for CRM selection
Why: Distant timeline (18+ months), low engagement (lukewarm receptiveness, 'can't promise I'll read it'), unclear authority, and no budget discussion make this a long-term prospect with minimal near-term opportunity.
